channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Defense chiefs from around 30 countries meet at NATO for talks on a future Ukraine force
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Britain's defense minister on Thursday urged military leaders from around 30 countries to press ahead with plans to deploy troops to Ukraine to police any future peace agreement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: EU putting tariff countermeasures on hold for 90 days after Trump pause
item.description: The European Union (EU) is putting its retaliatory tariffs on hold for 90 days after President Trump instituted a pause on his sweeping duties for most U.S. trading partners, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen unveiled early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian who took part in attack aged 13 and developed mental illness in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday released a Palestinian who took part in an attack when he was 13 and developed schizophrenia in prison as requests for early release were denied. Israel says Ahmad Manasra is a terrorist who tried to kill Jews his own age.
item.dc:creator: MAHMOUD ILLEAN and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US stocks slide, global markets soar in wake of Trump's tariff pause
item.description: World markets skyrocketed Thursday in the wake of President Donald Trump's 90-day retaliatory tariff pause.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Taliban morality enforcers arrest men for having the wrong hairstyle or skipping mosque, UN says
item.description: The Taliban morality police in Afghanistan have detained men and their barbers over hairstyles and others for missing prayers at mosques during the holy month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s Iran talks raise big questions on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Trump is set to open direct talks with Iran this weekend in a high-stakes push for Tehran to give up its nuclear weapons ambitions, raising a chorus of questions and concerns from lawmakers in both parties.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A prisoner swap between Moscow and Washington frees Ksenia Karelina, a US-Russia dual national
item.description: Russia has freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S. Thursday's prisoner swap comes as the two countries work to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU pauses tariff retaliation for 90 days to match Trump move, holding out hope for talks
item.description: The European Union’s executive commission says it will put on hold for 90 days its retaliation measures against new U.S. tariffs to match President Donald Trump’s pause on his sweeping new tariffs and leave room for a negotiated solution.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Zealand lawmakers reject proposed law to redefine the country's founding Treaty of Waitangi
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — New Zealand lawmakers dealt an overwhelming defeat Thursday to a controversial proposed law seeking to redefine the country’s founding treaty between Māori tribes and the British Crown.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Number of dead in a roof collapse at a nightclub in Dominican Republic rises to 218
item.description: The number of dead in a roof collapse at an iconic nightclub in the Dominican Republic has surged to 218.
item.dc:creator: Martín Adames Alcántara And Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canadian PM: Negotiations with Trump on economic, security relationship to begin after election
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday that negotiations with President Trump on the U.S.-Canada economic and security relationships will start after the Canadian federal election.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: The United States and China are locked in a faceoff over tariffs. No one wants to blink first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The tariff fight between the world's two largest economies spiraled into greater peril Wednesday as President Donald Trump tried to narrow his global trade war into a direct — and risky — faceoff with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korean leader's sister denounces denuclearization calls, saying nuclear status is permanent
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un on Wednesday mocked Washington and its Asian allies for what she called their “daydream” of denuclearizing the North, insisting that the country will never give up its nuclear weapons program.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congo repatriates 3 Americans who had faced death sentences over a failed coup plot
item.description: LAGOS, Nigeria (AP) — The three Americans convicted of participating in a botched coup attempt in Congo last year have been repatriated to the United States, days after their death sentences were commuted to life imprisonment, Congo's presidency said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: The roof of an iconic nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic collapsed nearly an hour after a merengue concert began, killing at least 218 people and injuring over 200 more.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Once a migratory highway, the Darien Gap has been left empty under Trump crackdown
item.description: LAJAS BLANCAS, Panama (AP) — A little more than a year ago, the small Panamanian river port of Lajas Blancas was filled with a crush of people trying to make their way to the United States. Now, the makeshift migrant camp has become a ghost town.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 18 dead, 120 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: A roof collapse at Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic killed 18 and injured 120. Rescue teams are searching for survivors, while the cause remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu to meet at White House as Israel seeks tariff relief, discussions on Iran, Gaza hostages
item.description: President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu set for White House meeting to discuss ending U.S. tariffs on Israel, the war in Gaza, the release of hostages and Iran's nuclear threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian air strikes kill 1 in Kyiv as Zelenskyy demands more pressure on Putin
item.description: One person was killed in Russian air strikes on Kyiv on Sunday while Kryvyi Rih mourns 19 victims of Friday's attack, which local authorities say also destroyed dozens of homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Stellacci, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Belgium charges 8 people in European Union Parliament bribery probe
item.description: Following a probe into suspected bribery at the European Parliament, eight people have been charged with money laundering, corruption and participation in a criminal organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
